invariant: Visual state, operability, accessibility-tree exposure, and focus must describe the same current UI state.

patterns.title: Modal dialog, drawer, or full-screen popup
patterns.applicability: Opening the surface makes the rest of the page unavailable until the surface closes.

checks.title: The modal is exposed as one named dialog
checks.expectation: The open surface has dialog semantics and a useful accessible name; descriptive text is associated only when it remains concise.
code_inspection: Inspect the rendered dialog role and computed accessible name rather than relying on component names.
runtime_verification: Open the surface and confirm the target screen reader announces the dialog and its useful name once.
cant_tell_when: The computed role or name cannot be inspected in the target browser.

checks.title: Closed modal content is absent
checks.expectation: When closed, dialog content and its controls are absent from sequential focus and the accessibility tree.
code_inspection: Check conditional rendering, hidden state, inertness, and focusability; opacity or off-screen positioning alone is insufficient.
runtime_verification: Before opening, navigate with keyboard and screen-reader commands and confirm no dialog descendant is encountered.
cant_tell_when: The closed state or accessibility tree cannot be captured.

checks.title: Opening moves focus into the modal
checks.expectation: Opening places focus at a deliberate element inside the dialog where the task and context are understandable.
code_inspection: Trace every opening path, including responsive and selection-triggered paths, to its initial-focus behavior.
runtime_verification: Open through every trigger and record the active element and first screen-reader announcement.
cant_tell_when: An opening path cannot be operated without prohibited state change.

checks.title: Background content is unavailable while open
checks.expectation: Page content outside the modal is inert and not exposed as available interaction while the modal is open.
code_inspection: Inspect the modal implementation's background isolation, including portals and sibling roots.
runtime_verification: With the modal open, use keyboard and screen-reader navigation to confirm background controls cannot be reached.
cant_tell_when: The complete page root or portal relationship cannot be inspected.

checks.title: Keyboard focus remains within the modal
checks.expectation: Tab and Shift+Tab cycle through the modal without entering background content until the modal closes.
code_inspection: Inspect focus-trap behavior and all focusable descendants, including conditionally rendered controls.
runtime_verification: Traverse forward and backward beyond both focus boundaries and record the active element sequence.
cant_tell_when: The rendered focusable set is incomplete or the interaction cannot be performed.

checks.title: Escape closes the modal
checks.expectation: Escape closes the modal unless a documented task-specific exception is necessary.
code_inspection: Inspect keyboard handlers and library defaults for nested or composed surfaces.
runtime_verification: Press Escape from representative controls and confirm the same close transition occurs.
cant_tell_when: Escape behavior depends on an untestable nested state.

checks.title: Closing returns focus to a logical origin
checks.expectation: After close, focus returns to the opening control or another logical continuation when that control no longer exists.
code_inspection: Trace close-by-button, Escape, overlay, submit, and responsive-transition paths.
runtime_verification: Close through each supported route and record the resulting active element.
cant_tell_when: The opening control is destroyed and no documented continuation target is available.

patterns.title: Disclosure or hamburger navigation
patterns.applicability: A button shows or hides ordinary page content without creating a modal interaction context.

checks.title: The trigger is a named button
checks.expectation: The show/hide control is keyboard-operable and its accessible name communicates its purpose.
code_inspection: Prefer a native button and inspect its computed accessible name.
runtime_verification: Reach the trigger by keyboard and screen-reader navigation and activate it with Enter and Space.
cant_tell_when: The trigger's computed role or name cannot be inspected.

checks.title: Expanded state matches visibility
checks.expectation: The trigger's expanded state changes with the visual and accessibility-tree visibility of the controlled content.
code_inspection: Inspect the state source, aria-expanded value, controlled content, and optional aria-controls reference in both states.
runtime_verification: Toggle twice and record visual state, computed expanded state, and announced state after each activation.
cant_tell_when: Only one of the open or closed states can be inspected.

checks.title: Collapsed content is not encountered
checks.expectation: Collapsed descendants are absent from sequential focus and screen-reader navigation.
code_inspection: Check the actual rendered hidden mechanism; visual clipping or opacity alone is insufficient.
runtime_verification: Navigate past the collapsed trigger and confirm hidden descendants are not encountered.
cant_tell_when: The collapsed accessibility tree cannot be observed.

patterns.title: Menu button and composite popup
patterns.applicability: A button opens a menu of actions that uses menu and menuitem interaction semantics; ordinary site navigation is usually a disclosure instead.

checks.title: Menu semantics fit the interaction
checks.expectation: Menu and menuitem roles are used only when the popup implements the corresponding composite-widget keyboard model.
code_inspection: Distinguish an application action menu from ordinary navigation links before selecting the pattern.
runtime_verification: Confirm the target screen reader identifies the control and popup in a way that matches the implemented keyboard behavior.
cant_tell_when: The product's intended interaction model is undocumented.

checks.title: Opening state and focus move together
checks.expectation: Opening updates the trigger state and moves focus to the intended menu item or managed active descendant.
code_inspection: Inspect aria-haspopup, aria-expanded, popup naming, and the chosen focus-management strategy.
runtime_verification: Open by each supported key and record the announced state and focused or active item.
cant_tell_when: Managed focus or active-descendant state cannot be observed.

checks.title: Closing removes the popup and returns focus
checks.expectation: Escape closes the popup, removes its items from navigation, and returns focus to the menu button.
code_inspection: Trace close and item-activation paths and inspect the closed rendered state.
runtime_verification: Close with Escape and after representative activation, then record popup exposure and active element.
cant_tell_when: The activation path performs a prohibited or irreversible action.

patterns.title: Visually fragmented logical text
patterns.applicability: One logical phrase, such as a number and unit, is split into styled visual fragments and is reported or observed as separate navigation or reading units.

checks.title: One equivalent phrase is exposed
checks.expectation: Assistive technology receives one complete phrase while decorative visual fragments are not exposed twice.
code_inspection: Inspect the hidden complete phrase, aria-hidden visual group, and absence of interactive descendants in that group.
runtime_verification: Navigate by the affected granularity and confirm the phrase is encountered once as a meaningful unit.
cant_tell_when: No reported fragmentation or target assistive-technology requirement can be reproduced or established.

checks.title: Visual and assistive text share one data source
checks.expectation: Visible fragments and the assistive phrase are derived from the same value and formatting rules.
code_inspection: Trace updates, signs, separators, units, locale variants, and responsive variants to a shared input.
runtime_verification: Change representative values and confirm both visual and assistive representations update consistently.
cant_tell_when: The rendering inputs or formatting rules are unavailable.

checks.title: Spoken pronunciation remains a runtime result
checks.expectation: Pronunciation is verified with the target screen reader, voice, browser, version, and locale rather than inferred from source or the accessibility tree.
code_inspection: Record structure as verified separately from pronunciation and do not promote the former into the latter.
runtime_verification: Record the exact spoken result and environment for representative values and punctuation.
cant_tell_when: The target screen reader, voice, browser, version, or locale is unavailable.
